$doc: BMD-derived input→output pairs for the Lots on Delay metric. Used by both ehc_contracts.metrics.lots_on_delay and by the Board Auditor's independent replica in ehc-data-analysis/data_board_auditor/tests/test_auditor_bmd_parity.py. Each case provides per-lot-type P and F aggregates (what the emitters produce after their Julian_Date filter) plus ph_lots (current-month qs_Plat_Hiatus.Lots sum). Expected outputs are (is_behind, abs_neg_variance) from compute_lot_variance and the per-project int from lots_on_delay_count.

cases.name: zero_variance_not_behind
cases.description: All lot types match P exactly. Not behind, zero delay lots. Rule 3.

cases.name: accelerated_only_not_behind
cases.description: Every lot type is ahead of schedule. Not behind — positive variance never counts as delayed. Rule 3.

cases.name: one_lot_type_behind_no_ph
cases.description: Behind on one lot type, no plat/hiatus activity. Rule 2 — return abs(variance).

cases.name: one_lot_type_behind_with_ph
cases.description: Behind on one lot type, plat/hiatus activity present. Rule 1 — ph_lots wins even if smaller than variance.

cases.name: behind_with_ph_larger_than_variance
cases.description: Behind on one lot type by 1, plat/hiatus reports 5 lots. Rule 1 — ph_lots wins and the number can exceed the variance (plat/hiatus measures the contractual delay, not the cumulative miss).

cases.name: multiple_lot_types_behind
cases.description: Behind on three lot types simultaneously — variance sums as absolute negatives.

cases.name: mixed_positive_and_negative_net_zero
cases.description: Behind on one lot type by 3, ahead on another by 3. Net total variance is zero but lot-type-level rule says BEHIND — abs_neg_variance = 3 only (positive variance excluded). This is the subtle case where summing lot-type variances before taking abs produces the wrong answer.

cases.name: mixed_positive_and_negative_net_negative
cases.description: Behind on one lot type by 4, ahead on another by 1. Behind; abs_neg_variance = 4.

cases.name: fractional_ph_lots_rounds_to_nearest
cases.description: ph_lots = 2.5 rounds to 2 (banker's rounding in Python's round()). Rule 1 applies; verify int(round(...)) is used, not int(...) or math.floor.

cases.name: fractional_variance_rounds_to_nearest
cases.description: abs_neg_variance = 2.7 from fractional F takedown — Rule 2 returns int(round(2.7)) = 3. V3 cashflow lot columns are typically whole numbers but the contract tolerates floats.

cases.name: all_lot_cols_empty
cases.description: Degenerate case — P and F both zero on every lot type. Not behind. Rule 3.

cases.name: f_dict_missing_key_treated_as_zero
cases.description: P has all 7 lot types; F dict omits mnth_C_Lots entirely (absent key, not 0). Must treat missing as 0 and flag BEHIND if P_C > 0. Mirrors the emitter pandas case where a lot-type column may not be present in cf_f_valid.

cases.name: none_values_treated_as_zero
cases.description: pandas sum of empty series yields NaN in some paths; callers may pre-coerce or pass None. The shared function must treat None as 0.0 to match the emitter fallback behavior.

cases.name: behind_but_ph_lots_zero_rule_2_applies
cases.description: Explicit check that ph_lots == 0 routes to Rule 2, not Rule 1. Rule 1 requires ph_lots > 0 strictly.

invalid_cases.name: negative_abs_neg_variance_raises
invalid_cases.description: abs_neg_variance is defined as ≥ 0 (sum of absolute values of negative deltas). A negative input is a caller bug; the function raises ValueError.

invalid_cases.name: negative_ph_lots_raises
invalid_cases.description: qs_Plat_Hiatus.Lots is a lot count and cannot be negative. A negative here indicates a sign-convention error upstream.
